subject: Physics
title: Einstein's Biggest Mistake
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Einstein's Biggest Mistake.

hook.text: Einstein added a fudge factor to his equations to keep the universe static. He called it his biggest blunder.

buildup.text: In 1917, Einstein believed the universe was eternal and unchanging—like most scientists of his era.

discovery.text: He added the cosmological constant to prevent his equations from predicting an expanding universe.

twist.text: When Hubble proved the universe is expanding in 1929, Einstein removed the constant in embarrassment.

climax.text: In 1998, dark energy was discovered—and it behaves exactly like Einstein's cosmological constant.

punchline.text: His biggest blunder turned out to be right. Even his mistakes were brilliant.

quiz.question: Why did Einstein originally add the cosmological constant?
options: To prevent his equations from predicting an expanding universe
To account for the speed of light
